workout.title: Moderate Long Run (Time)
workout.desc: This moderate-effort long run develops aerobic endurance and muscular resilience while reinforcing steady pacing near marathon intensity. The consistent rhythm helps you refine control and efficiency, training both the body and mind to sustain effort over time. You should feel smooth, strong, and composed—focused on maintaining a steady flow rather than chasing speed.

workout.title: Base Builder
workout.desc: This aerobic endurance and speed development workout blends steady effort running with short, fast strides to build a strong foundation. The goal is to reinforce aerobic efficiency while introducing light neuromuscular work to improve leg turnover and running economy. You should feel smooth and controlled during the steady effort, then quick and snappy during the faster bursts.

workout.title: Middle Peak
workout.desc: This fartlek pyramid workout is designed to build aerobic power, improve lactate clearance, and sharpen your ability to change gears under control. The effort gradually rises from steady threshold running into short, high-intensity surges, then returns to strong but sustainable running. You should feel smooth and controlled early, progressively challenged through the middle, and sharply engaged during the hardest segments. Finish feeling accomplished, knowing you've trained both durability and top-end efficiency in one session.
